meta.feed_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

items.title: Mass wedding brings brief joy to couples in devastated Gaza
items.summary: Palestinian couples gathered for a mass wedding ceremony in Gaza organised amid rubble and displacement.

items.title: Social media becomes a ‘goldmine’ for fraudsters in Jordan
items.summary: Jordanians looking for a bargain are being scammed with false promises of cheap gold in a growing trend of online fraud.

items.title: Israeli military kills six in Lebanon, issues more displacement threats
items.summary: The deadly air attack comes just days before Israeli and Lebanese officials are due to meet in Washington.

items.title: Live: First UK minister quits amid calls for PM Starmer’s resignation
items.summary: The British premier is under pressure to quit after his Labour Party suffered election losses.

items.title: Zelenskyy says Russia fired over 200 drones at Ukraine as truce expires
items.summary: One killed and four others wounded in attacks on Ukraine's Dnipropetrovsk region, local administration chief says.

items.title: France’s Macron announces $27bn investment in Africa at Kenya summit
items.summary: Africa Forward summit comes amid France's fading ties with its former African colonies, as Kenya seeks greater trade.

items.title: ‘Whole morning revolves around gas’: Pakistan’s fuel crisis enters kitchens
items.summary: The crisis sees women waking up early, cooking quickly, rearranging meals and planning days around the prospect of gas.

items.title: Trump and Xi: The history of encounters between two superpower leaders
items.summary: US President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping have met six times since 2017.

items.title: Gunman arrested after opening fire on busy street near Harvard University
items.summary: Gunman arrested after opening fire on busy street near Harvard University

items.title: Starmer to face split cabinet as demands for his resignation mount
items.summary: The UK prime minister is so far defiant in the face of calls for him to step down.

items.title: Mexico cancels plan to cut school year for World Cup after backlash
items.summary: Officials agree to keep the school calendar as originally planned, ending on July 15 and resuming on August 31.

items.title: Palestine weekly wrap: Israeli settlers rampage through West Bank villages
items.summary: Settlers uproot olive trees, seize land, and displace families, reflecting unchecked violence across the West Bank.

items.title: ‘Potential security risk’: Unpacking the UK’s trust issues with Palantir
items.summary: Analysts say it is challenging to track whether Palantir is sticking to the terms of its NHS contract.

items.title: Starving on the front lines: Food supply in crisis as Ukraine fights Russia
items.summary: Photos of emaciated Ukrainians reveal conditions suffered by Ukraine, as meagre rations also afflict Russian soldiers.

items.title: Why is Israel allowed to take part in the Eurovision song contest?
items.summary: Five countries are boycotting the 70th edition of Eurovision on May 16 in protest over Israel's genocidal war on Gaza.

items.title: Iran war: What’s happening on day 74 as Tehran says ready for ‘aggression’
items.summary: US-Iran tensions remain high as Tehran warns against 'aggression' and Trump says the ceasefire is on 'life support'.

items.title: Malaysia rescues 23 migrants after boat capsizes, 14 still missing
items.summary: Authorities deploy boats, helicopter and surveillance aircraft to search for missing migrants off Pangkor Island.

items.title: Thunder outlasts Lakers in Game 4 to complete NBA playoff sweep
items.summary: Reigning champions Oklahoma City Thunder remain undefeated in playoffs and move on to Western Conference finals.

items.title: Global energy crisis highlights meagre oil buffers in developing world
items.summary: Governments in the Global South are among the least prepared to respond to the oil shock caused by the Iran war.

items.title: US moves to release more oil stockpiles under IEA agreement
items.summary: US Department of Energy moves to transfer 53.3 million barrels amid rising oil prices.

items.title: Israel approves law on public trials, death penalty for October 7 detainees
items.summary: Rights groups warn that the bill makes the death penalty easier to impose and strips fair trial protections.

items.title: Hundreds more displaced as gang violence escalates in Haiti’s capital
items.summary: Renewed clashes between rival gangs in Port-au-Prince have forced hundreds to flee their homes.

items.title: Flotilla activist Thiago Ávila arrives in Brazil
items.summary: Brazilian activist Thiago Ávila returned to São Paulo after being detained and deported from Israel.

items.title: California ex-mayor admits acting as agent of China, US authorities say
items.summary: Ex-mayor of wealthy Los Angeles suburb promoted pro-China propaganda at behest of Chinese officials, prosecutors say.

items.title: Lebanese in south refuse to flee again despite escalating Israeli strikes
items.summary: Obaida Hitto reports from southern Lebanon, where residents say they will not leave again despite intensifying strikes.
